content_en:
Regarding men and women, yes, it is confirmed for women and for men. This is because the basic principle is that men and women are the same in the rulings of Islamic Law, unless there is some legal evidence to indicate otherwise. There is no authentic legal proof that a woman performs her prayer different than a man; in fact she and the man are equal in this matter.

Q. 257. An Imam makes the Taslim by saying As-Salamu ‘Alaykum only once; is it sufficient to restrict oneself to one salutation? Advise us, and may Allah reward you with goodness.

A. Some of the scholars consider it permissible to content oneself with one salutation, others consider that it is essential to make two salutations, while others hold that one salutation is sufficient in the supererogatory prayers, but not the obligatory ones.

The most prudent thing is to make two Taslims, because this has been reported more than anything else from the Prophet 2%, and it is more prudent, and involves mentioning Allah more.

But if the Imam made the Taslim once and the one being led in

prayer did not consider it sufficient to make only one salutation,

then he may make the Taslim twice and there is no sin upon _ him in doing this.

If the Imam made two Taslims, and the one being led in prayer

considers that one Taslim is sufficient, he should make Taslim

with the Jmam in order to follow the Imam.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 490 peY! ols yt ws

Q. 258. Is it more fitting for the Imam to leave directly after the prayer, or should he wait a while?

A, It is more fitting for the Imam to wait, facing the Qiblah for the time it takes to seek forgiveness from Allah three times and to say:

“Oh, Allah, You are Peace and from You comes peace, Most Blessed are You, oh, Owner of Majesty and Honor.”

Then he should turn to face those whom he led in prayer.!"!

As for remaining in his place, if his standing will necessitate him treading on the necks of the worshippers, then it is better for him to remain until he finds space; if not, then he may leave.

As for the one led in prayer, it is better that he does not leave before his Imam, according to the words of the Prophet #:

“Do not anticipate me in leaving.’"*!

However, if the mam remained for a long time facing the Qiblah longer than the Sunnah, then the one led in prayer may leave.

\") This is the Hadith of Thawban reported by Muslim in the Book of the Masjids, in Chapter 26: The Recommendation to Utter Remembrance After the Prayer 1:414, Hadith 135 (591).

'2] Reported by Muslim in the Book of Prayer, in Chapter 25: The Forbiddance of Anticipating the Imam 1:320, Hadith 112 (426); and at the beginning of it: “Oh, you people! I am your Imam, so do not anticipate me in Ruku’, nor in Sujud, nor in standing, nor in leaving.”

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 491 Beall sold

Q. 259. What is the opinion of Your Eminence regarding shaking hands and saying: Taqabbal Allah (May Allah accept your prayers) immediately after leaving the prayer, may Allah reward you with goodness?

A. There is no basis for shaking hands, nor for saying Tagabbal Allah after completing the prayer, because it has not been reported from the Prophet #¢, nor has it been reported from his Companions, may Allah be pleased with them.

Q. 260. What is your opinion regarding the use of Misbahah''! when making Tasbih, may Allah reward you with goodness?

A. The use of Sibhah (i.e. Misbahah) is permissible, but it is better to make Tasbih by counting on the finger-joints, because the Prophet 2% said:

“Count on the fingers, because they will be questioned.’'!

And (they are also not recommended) because there might be something of show in them, and because in most cases, when a person makes Tasbih using Sibhah, one finds that his heart is not present, so he makes Tasbih with the Misbahah and he looks to right and left. So the fingers are better and they are more fitting.

Q. 261. What are the statements of remembrance that are legislated after making the Taslim in the prayer?

A. Mentioning Allah, the Most High after the prayers was

''l Misbahah: beads to count statements of remembrance with.

'2] Reported by Imam Ahmad in Al-Musnad 6:370; by Abu Dawud in the Book of Prayer, in the Chapter: Tasbeeh Using Stones (1501); and by AtTirmithi in the Book of Supplications, in the Chapter: The Virtues of Tasbih (3583) and the wording of the Hadith is: Count on the fingerjoints, because they will be asked and questioned.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 492 PEM Olah sts

commanded by Allah in His Words:

wesc Bho ee cay phe’ (363

Ca Ges OS al eet Lat As “When you have finished As-salah (the congregational prayer), remember Allah standing, sitting down, and (lying down) on your sides.""") And these statements of remembrance, that Allah has commanded in general, have been made clear by the Prophet 32, who said:

“When you make the Taslim, seek forgiveness from Allah three times.” (say:) MH da obs GV LY YG

NG ject tg ge Y pall ak aa Bb sha tal

LY abl Yo Ue

(Allahumma Antas-Salamu Wa Minkas-Salamu, Tabarakta Ya Thul-Jalali Wal-Ikram. La Maha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul-Hamdu Wa Huwa ‘Ala Kulli Shay'in Qadeer Allahumma La Mani‘a Lima A‘taita Wa La Mu'tiya Lima Mana‘ta Wa La Yanfa‘u Thal-Jaddi Minkal-Jadd. La Ilahah Ilallahu Wa La Na‘budu Illa lyyahu, LahunNi‘matu Wa Lahul-Fadhlu Wa Lahuth-Thana ul-Hasan. La Hawla Wa La Quwwata Illa Billahi, La aha Illallahu Wa La Na‘budu Illa Iyyahu Mukhliseena Lahud-Deenu Wa Law

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 493 BMI cod

“Oh, Allah, You are Peace and from You comes peace. Blessed are You, Oh, Owner of majesty and honour!"! None has the right to be worshipped but Allah Alone, He has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is able to do all things. Oh, Allah, there is none who can withhold what You have given and none may give what You have withheld, and the might of a might of a mighty person cannot benefit him against You.!?! None has the right to be worshipped but Allah, and we do not worship any other besides Him. His is grace, and His is bounty and to Him belongs the most excellent Praise. There is no power and no might except by Allah. None has the right to be worshipped but Allah and we do not worship any other besides Him (we are) sincere in making our religious devotion to HIm, even though the disbelievers may dislike it.’">)

You should glorify Allah (Tasbih) by what has been reported from the Prophet #%, such as glorifying Allah, praising Him (Tahmid) and making Takbir, each of them thirty-three times, saying: Subhan Allah and Al-Hamdu Lillah and Allahu Akbar thirty-three times, then completing the hundred by saying: La Tlaha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul-

"1 Reported by Muslim in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Recommendation of Uttering Statements of Remembrance After Prayer (595).

1 This wording is agreed upon (by Al-Bukhari and Muslim) in the Hadith of Al-Mughirah bin Shu’bah, may Allah be pleased with him; it was reported by Al-Bukhari in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: The Remembrance After Prayer (844) and in the Book of Supplications, in the Chapter: The Supplication After Prayer (6329); and in other Books and it was reported by Muslim in the Book of Masjids, in Chapter 26: The Recommendation of Uttering Statements of Remembrance After Prayer 1:414, Hadith 137 (593).

'9) This is the Hadith of ‘Abdullah bin Zubair, may Allah be pleased with him, reported by Muslim in the aforementioned location, Hadith 139

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 494 ey OlS yh Gat

Hamdu Wa Huwa ‘Ala Kulli Shay’in Qadeer.

It is the same whether you said it all together: Subhan Allahi WalHamdu Lillahi Wallahu Akbar thirty-three times, or you said the Tasbih by itself, the Tahmid by itself and the Takbir by itself and then completed it by saying: La Taha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul-Hamdu Wa Huwa ‘Ala Kulli Shay’in Qadeer.

Likewise, it is permissible to make Tasbih, Tahmid and Takbir ten at a time, instead of making them thirty-three at a time, by saying: Subhan Allah ten times, Al-Hamdu Lillah ten times and Allahu Akbar ten times, which comes to thirty; and this is what has been reported in the Sunnah.)

It has also been reported in the Sunnah that one may say Subhan Allahi Wal-Hamdy Lillahi Wa La Ilaha Illallahu Wallahu Akbar, these four things are said twenty-five times, making the total one hundred.!?!

So, whichever of these forms you used to make Tasbih, it is permissible, because the rule of the Islamic Law is that if acts of worship have been reported in various forms, it is a Sunnah to perform all of them, once this and once that, in order that a person may perform the Sunnah in all its forms. These statements of remembrance which I mentioned are general for all of the prayers: Fajr, Zuhr, ‘Asr, Maghrib and ‘Isha’. And in

"1 Reported by Abu Dawud in the Book of Good Manners, in the Chapter Regarding Tasbih at the Time of Sleeping, Hadith (5065); and it was reported by At-Tirmithi in the Book of Supplications, in Chapter 25, Hadith (3410); and by An-Nasa’i in the Book of Forgetfulness, in the Chapter: The Number of Tasbih After the Taslim (1347); and by Ibn Majah in the Book of Establishing the Prayer, in the Chapter: What is Said After the Taslim (926).

'] Reported by At-Tirmithi in the Book of Supplications, in Chapter 25 (3413) and he declared it to be authentic and it was reported by AnNasa’i in the Book of Forgetfulness, in Chapter 93: Another Form of

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 495 BLAS! sgl

Maghrib and Fajr, the Tahlil''! is performed ten times, and likewise the words Rabbi Ajirni Min An-Nar (My Lord! Grant me protection from the Fire) are repeated seven times after the Maghrib and the Fajr prayers. And Allah is the Granter of success.

Q. 262. What is the ruling on raising the hands and supplicating after the prayer?

A. It is not legislated for a person to raise his hands and supplicate after completing the prayer. If he wishes to supplicate, then supplication during the prayer is better than him supplicating after he finishes it. This is why the Prophet # indicated that in the Hadith of Ibn Mas’ud, may Allah be pleased with him, in which he mentioned the Tashahhud, saying:
content_id:
Mengenai laki-laki dan perempuan, ya, hal ini berlaku bagi perempuan dan juga bagi laki-laki. Ini karena prinsip dasarnya adalah bahwa laki-laki dan perempuan sama dalam hukum-hukum syariat Islam, kecuali ada dalil syar'i yang menunjukkan sebaliknya. Tidak ada dalil sahih yang menunjukkan bahwa seorang perempuan melaksanakan salatnya berbeda dari laki-laki; bahkan ia dan laki-laki setara dalam hal ini.

T. 257. Seorang imam mengucapkan salam hanya sekali; apakah cukup membatasi diri dengan satu salam saja? Berilah kami nasihat, semoga Allah membalas kebaikan Anda.

J. Sebagian ulama menganggap boleh mencukupkan diri dengan satu salam saja, yang lain menganggap bahwa dua salam itu wajib, sementara yang lainnya berpendapat bahwa satu salam cukup dalam salat sunnah tetapi tidak dalam salat wajib.

Yang paling hati-hati adalah mengucapkan dua salam, karena hal ini lebih banyak diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ, dan itu lebih berhati-hati serta mengandung lebih banyak dzikir kepada Allah.

Namun jika imam mengucapkan salam sekali saja dan makmum tidak menganggap satu salam itu cukup,

maka ia boleh mengucapkan salam dua kali dan tidak ada dosa baginya dalam hal ini.

Jika imam mengucapkan dua salam, dan makmum

menganggap satu salam sudah cukup, ia tetap harus mengucapkan salam

bersama imam demi mengikuti imam.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 490 peY! ols yt ws

T. 258. Apakah lebih baik bagi imam untuk langsung pergi setelah salat, atau sebaiknya menunggu sebentar?

J. Lebih baik bagi imam untuk menunggu, menghadap kiblat selama waktu yang diperlukan untuk beristighfar kepada Allah tiga kali dan mengucapkan:

"Ya Allah, Engkau adalah As-Salam dan dari-Mu datang keselamatan, Maha Berkah Engkau, wahai Pemilik Keagungan dan Kemuliaan."

Kemudian ia hendaknya berbalik menghadap orang-orang yang dipimpinnya dalam salat.

Adapun tetap berada di tempatnya, jika berdirinya akan mengharuskannya melangkahi leher-leher jamaah, maka lebih baik baginya untuk tetap tinggal sampai ia menemukan ruang; jika tidak, maka ia boleh pergi.

Adapun makmum, lebih baik ia tidak pergi sebelum imamnya, berdasarkan sabda Nabi ﷺ:

"Janganlah kalian mendahului aku dalam pergi."

Namun, jika imam tetap menghadap kiblat dalam waktu yang lama melebihi yang disunnahkan, maka makmum boleh pergi.

¹⁾ Ini adalah hadits Tsauban yang diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid-Masjid, Bab 26: Anjuran Mengucapkan Dzikir Setelah Salat 1:414, Hadits 135 (591).

²⁾ Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Salat, Bab 25: Larangan Mendahului Imam 1:320, Hadits 112 (426); dan di awalnya: "Wahai manusia! Aku adalah imam kalian, maka janganlah kalian mendahului aku dalam ruku', dalam sujud, dalam berdiri, maupun dalam pergi."

Fatawa Relating to Prayer 491 Beall sold

T. 259. Apa pendapat Yang Mulia mengenai berjabat tangan dan mengucapkan: Taqabbal Allah (Semoga Allah menerima salat Anda) segera setelah selesai salat, semoga Allah membalas kebaikan Anda?

J. Tidak ada dasar untuk berjabat tangan, maupun mengucapkan Taqabbal Allah setelah menyelesaikan salat, karena hal itu tidak diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ, dan juga tidak diriwayatkan dari para sahabatnya, semoga Allah meridhai mereka.

T. 260. Apa pendapat Anda mengenai penggunaan misbahah (tasbih) ketika bertasbih, semoga Allah membalas kebaikan Anda?

J. Penggunaan sibhah (yaitu misbahah) itu diperbolehkan, tetapi lebih baik bertasbih dengan menghitung pada ruas-ruas jari, karena Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Hitunglah dengan jari-jari, karena jari-jari itu akan ditanya."

Dan (misbahah juga tidak dianjurkan) karena mungkin ada unsur riya di dalamnya, dan karena dalam kebanyakan kasus, ketika seseorang bertasbih menggunakan sibhah, hatinya tidak hadir, sehingga ia bertasbih dengan misbahah sambil menoleh ke kanan dan ke kiri. Maka jari-jari itu lebih baik dan lebih tepat.

T. 261. Apa saja dzikir-dzikir yang disyariatkan setelah mengucapkan salam dalam salat?

J. Berdzikir kepada Allah Yang Maha Tinggi setelah salat telah

¹⁾ Misbahah: butiran-butiran untuk menghitung dzikir.

²⁾ Diriwayatkan oleh Imam Ahmad dalam Al-Musnad 6:370; oleh Abu Dawud dalam Kitab Salat, Bab: Tasbih Menggunakan Batu Kerikil (1501); dan oleh At-Tirmidzi dalam Kitab Du'a, Bab: Keutamaan Tasbih (3583) dan lafaz haditsnya adalah: Hitunglah dengan ruas-ruas jari, karena ruas-ruas itu akan ditanya dan dimintai pertanggungjawaban.

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 492 PEM Olah sts

diperintahkan oleh Allah dalam firman-Nya:

wesc Bho ee cay phe' (363

Ca Ges OS al eet Lat As "Maka apabila kamu telah menyelesaikan salat (berjamaah), ingatlah Allah di waktu berdiri, di waktu duduk, dan di waktu berbaring." Dan dzikir-dzikir ini, yang Allah perintahkan secara umum, telah dijelaskan oleh Nabi ﷺ, yang bersabda:

"Apabila kamu mengucapkan salam, beristighfarlah kepada Allah tiga kali." (ucapkanlah:) MH da obs GV LY YG

La Ilaha Illallahu Wa La Na'budu Illa Iyyahu, LahunNi'matu Wa Lahul-Fadhlu Wa Lahuth-Thana ul-Hasan. Fatawa Relating to Prayer 493 BMI cod

"Ya Allah, Engkau adalah As-Salam dan dari-Mu datang keselamatan. Maha Berkah Engkau, wahai Pemilik Keagungan dan Kemuliaan! Tiada tuhan yang berhak disembah selain Allah semata, tiada sekutu bagi-Nya, milik-Nya kerajaan dan milik-Nya segala pujian, dan Dia Maha Kuasa atas segala sesuatu. Ya Allah, tidak ada yang dapat menghalangi apa yang Engkau berikan dan tidak ada yang dapat memberi apa yang Engkau halangi, dan kekayaan orang yang kaya tidak berguna baginya di hadapan-Mu. Tiada tuhan yang berhak disembah selain Allah, dan kami tidak menyembah selain Dia. Milik-Nya nikmat, milik-Nya karunia, dan bagi-Nya pujian yang baik. Tiada daya dan tiada kekuatan kecuali dengan Allah. Tiada tuhan yang berhak disembah selain Allah dan kami tidak menyembah selain Dia (kami) dengan mengikhlaskan ibadah kami kepada-Nya, meskipun orang-orang kafir membencinya."

Hendaklah engkau bertasbih kepada Allah dengan apa yang diriwayatkan dari Nabi ﷺ, seperti bertasbih kepada Allah, bertahmid kepada-Nya dan bertakbir, masing-masing tiga puluh tiga kali, mengucapkan: Subhan Allah dan Al-Hamdu Lillah dan Allahu Akbar tiga puluh tiga kali, kemudian menggenapkan seratus dengan mengucapkan: La Ilaha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul-

¹⁾ Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid-Masjid, Bab: Anjuran Mengucapkan Dzikir Setelah Salat (595).

²⁾ Lafaz ini disepakati (oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim) dalam hadits Al-Mughirah bin Syu'bah, semoga Allah meridhainya; diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dalam Kitab Adzan, Bab: Dzikir Setelah Salat (844) dan dalam Kitab Du'a, Bab: Du'a Setelah Salat (6329); serta dalam kitab-kitab lainnya. Diriwayatkan juga oleh Muslim dalam Kitab Masjid-Masjid, Bab 26: Anjuran Mengucapkan Dzikir Setelah Salat 1:414, Hadits 137 (593).

³⁾ Ini adalah hadits 'Abdullah bin Zubair, semoga Allah meridhainya, diriwayatkan oleh Muslim di lokasi yang disebutkan di atas, Hadits 139

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 494 ey OlS yh Gat

Hamdu Wa Huwa 'Ala Kulli Shay'in Qadeer.

Sama saja apakah engkau mengucapkannya sekaligus: Subhan Allahi Wal-Hamdu Lillahi Wallahu Akbar tiga puluh tiga kali, atau engkau mengucapkan tasbih tersendiri, tahmid tersendiri, dan takbir tersendiri kemudian menggenapkannya dengan mengucapkan: La Ilaha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul-Hamdu Wa Huwa 'Ala Kulli Shay'in Qadeer.

Demikian juga, diperbolehkan untuk bertasbih, bertahmid, dan bertakbir sepuluh kali, sebagai ganti dari tiga puluh tiga kali, dengan mengucapkan: Subhan Allah sepuluh kali, Al-Hamdu Lillah sepuluh kali, dan Allahu Akbar sepuluh kali, yang berjumlah tiga puluh; dan inilah yang diriwayatkan dalam Sunnah.

Juga diriwayatkan dalam Sunnah bahwa seseorang boleh mengucapkan Subhan Allahi Wal-Hamdu Lillahi Wa La Ilaha Illallahu Wallahu Akbar, empat kalimat ini diucapkan dua puluh lima kali, sehingga totalnya seratus.

Jadi, bentuk mana pun yang engkau gunakan untuk bertasbih, itu diperbolehkan, karena kaidah dalam syariat Islam adalah bahwa jika ibadah-ibadah diriwayatkan dalam berbagai bentuk, maka disunnahkan untuk mengamalkan semuanya, sekali ini dan sekali itu, agar seseorang dapat mengamalkan Sunnah dalam segala bentuknya. Dzikir-dzikir yang aku sebutkan ini bersifat umum untuk semua salat: Fajr, Zhuhur, Ashar, Maghrib, dan Isya. Dan pada

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud dalam Kitab Adab, dalam Bab Mengenai Tasbih pada Waktu Tidur, Hadith (5065); dan diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmithi dalam Kitab Du'a, dalam Bab 25, Hadith (3410); dan oleh An-Nasa'i dalam Kitab Sahwi (Lupa), dalam Bab: Bilangan Tasbih Setelah Taslim (1347); dan oleh Ibn Majah dalam Kitab Mendirikan Salah, dalam Bab: Apa yang Diucapkan Setelah Taslim (926).

'] Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmithi dalam Kitab Du'a, dalam Bab 25 (3413) dan beliau menyatakannya sahih, dan diriwayatkan oleh An-Nasa'i dalam Kitab Sahwi (Lupa), dalam Bab 93: Bentuk Lain dari

Fatwa Berkaitan dengan Salah 495 BLAS! sgl

Maghrib dan Fajr, tahlil''! dibaca sepuluh kali, dan demikian juga ucapan Rabbi Ajirni Min An-Nar (Ya Tuhanku! Lindungilah aku dari api neraka) diulang tujuh kali setelah salah Maghrib dan Fajr. Dan Allah-lah Pemberi taufik.

T. 262. Apa hukum mengangkat tangan dan berdu'a setelah salah?

J. Tidak disyariatkan bagi seseorang untuk mengangkat tangannya dan berdu'a setelah menyelesaikan salah. Jika ia ingin berdu'a, maka berdu'a di dalam salah lebih baik daripada berdu'a setelah selesai salah. Inilah sebabnya Nabi ﷺ mengisyaratkan hal tersebut dalam hadith Ibn Mas'ud, semoga Allah meridhainya, di mana beliau menyebutkan tasyahhud, dengan mengatakan:
content_ar: Regarding men and women, yes, it is confirmed for women and for men. This is because the basic principle is that men and women are the same in the rulings of الإسلامي Law, unless there is some legal evidence to indicate otherwise. There is no صحيح legal proof that a woman performs her الصلاة different than a man; in fact she and the man are equal in this matter. Q. 257. An الإمام makes the Taslim by saying As-Salamu ‘Alaykum only once; is it sufficient to restrict oneself to one salutation? Advise us, and may الله reward you with goodness. A. Some of the العلماء consider it مباح to content oneself with one salutation, others consider that it is essential to make two salutations, while others hold that one salutation is sufficient in the supererogatory prayers, but not the واجب ones. The most prudent thing is to make two Taslims, because this has been reported more than anything else from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 2%, and it is more prudent, and involves mentioning الله more. But if the الإمام made the Taslim once and the one being led in الصلاة did not consider it sufficient to make only one salutation, then he may make the Taslim twice and there is no sin upon _ him in doing this. If the الإمام made two Taslims, and the one being led in الصلاة considers that one Taslim is sufficient, he should make Taslim with the Jmam in order to follow the الإمام. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 490 peY! ols yt ws Q. 258. Is it more fitting for the الإمام to leave directly after the الصلاة, or should he wait a while? A, It is more fitting for the الإمام to wait, facing the القبلة for the time it takes to seek forgiveness from الله three times and to say: “Oh, الله, You are Peace and from You comes peace, Most Blessed are You, oh, Owner of Majesty and Honor.” Then he should turn to face those whom he led in الصلاة.!"! As for remaining in his place, if his standing will necessitate him treading on the necks of the worshippers, then it is better for him to remain until he finds space; if not, then he may leave. As for the one led in الصلاة, it is better that he does not leave before his الإمام, according to the words of النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #: “Do not anticipate me in leaving.’"*! However, if the mam remained for a long time facing the القبلة longer than السنة النبوية, then the one led in الصلاة may leave. \") This is الحديث النبوي الشريف of Thawban reported by المسلم in the Book of the Masjids, in Chapter 26: The Recommendation to Utter Remembrance After the الصلاة 1:414, الحديث 135 (591). '2] Reported by المسلم in the Book of الصلاة, in Chapter 25: The Forbiddance of Anticipating the الإمام 1:320, الحديث 112 (426); and at the beginning of it: “Oh, you people! I am your الإمام, so do not anticipate me in Ruku’, nor in Sujud, nor in standing, nor in leaving.” الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 491 Beall sold Q. 259. What is the opinion of Your Eminence regarding shaking hands and saying: Taqabbal الله (May الله accept your prayers) immediately after leaving the الصلاة, may الله reward you with goodness? A. There is no basis for shaking hands, nor for saying Tagabbal الله after completing the الصلاة, because it has not been reported from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #¢, nor has it been reported from his الصحابة, رضي الله عنهم. Q. 260. What is your opinion regarding the use of Misbahah''! when making Tasbih, may الله reward you with goodness? A. The use of Sibhah (i.e. Misbahah) is مباح, but it is better to make Tasbih by counting on the finger-joints, because النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 2% said: “Count on the fingers, because they will be questioned.’'! And (they are also not مستحب) because there might be something of show in them, and because in most cases, when a person makes Tasbih using Sibhah, one finds that his heart is not present, so he makes Tasbih with the Misbahah and he looks to right and left. So the fingers are better and they are more fitting. Q. 261. What are the statements of remembrance that are legislated after making the Taslim in the الصلاة? A. Mentioning الله, the Most High after the prayers was ''l Misbahah: beads to count statements of remembrance with. '2] Reported by الإمام Ahmad in Al-Musnad 6:370; by أبو داود in the Book of الصلاة, in the Chapter: Tasbeeh Using Stones (1501); and by AtTirmithi in the Book of Supplications, in the Chapter: The Virtues of Tasbih (3583) and the wording of الحديث النبوي الشريف is: Count on the fingerjoints, because they will be asked and questioned. الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 492 PEM Olah sts commanded by الله in His Words: wesc Bho ee cay phe’ (363 Ca Ges OS al eet Lat As “When you have finished As-الصلاة (the congregational الصلاة), remember الله standing, sitting down, and (lying down) on your sides.""") And these statements of remembrance, that الله has commanded in general, have been made clear by النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم 32, who said: “When you make the Taslim, seek forgiveness from الله three times.” (say:) MH da obs GV LY YG NG ject tg ge Y pall ak aa Bb sha tal LY abl Yo Ue (Allahumma Antas-Salamu Wa Minkas-Salamu, Tabarakta Ya Thul-Jalali Wal-Ikram. La Maha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul-Hamdu Wa Huwa ‘Ala Kulli Shay'in Qadeer Allahumma La Mani‘a Lima A‘taita Wa La Mu'tiya Lima Mana‘ta Wa La Yanfa‘u Thal-Jaddi Minkal-Jadd. La Ilahah Ilallahu Wa La Na‘budu Illa lyyahu, LahunNi‘matu Wa Lahul-Fadhlu Wa Lahuth-Thana ul-Hasan. La Hawla Wa La Quwwata Illa Billahi, La aha Illallahu Wa La Na‘budu Illa Iyyahu Mukhliseena Lahud-Deenu Wa Law الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 493 BMI cod “Oh, الله, You are Peace and from You comes peace. Blessed are You, Oh, Owner of majesty and honour!"! None has the right to be worshipped but الله Alone, He has no partner, His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He is able to do all things. Oh, الله, there is none who can withhold what You have given and none may give what You have withheld, and the might of a might of a mighty person cannot benefit him against You.!?! None has the right to be worshipped but الله, and we do not worship any other besides Him. His is grace, and His is bounty and to Him belongs the most excellent Praise. There is no power and no might except by الله. None has the right to be worshipped but الله and we do not worship any other besides Him (we are) sincere in making our religious devotion to HIm, even though the disbelievers may dislike it.’">) You should glorify الله (Tasbih) by what has been reported from النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #%, such as glorifying الله, praising Him (Tahmid) and making Takbir, each of them thirty-three times, saying: Subhan الله and Al-Hamdu Lillah and Allahu Akbar thirty-three times, then completing the hundred by saying: La Tlaha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul- "1 Reported by المسلم in the Book of Masjids, in the Chapter: The Recommendation of Uttering Statements of Remembrance After الصلاة (595). 1 This wording is agreed upon (by البخاري and المسلم) in الحديث النبوي الشريف of Al-Mughirah bin Shu’bah, رضي الله عنه; it was reported by البخاري in the Book of the adhan, in the Chapter: The Remembrance After الصلاة (844) and in the Book of Supplications, in the Chapter: The الدعاء After الصلاة (6329); and in other Books and it was reported by المسلم in the Book of Masjids, in Chapter 26: The Recommendation of Uttering Statements of Remembrance After الصلاة 1:414, الحديث 137 (593). '9) This is الحديث النبوي الشريف of ‘Abdullah bin Zubair, رضي الله عنه, reported by المسلم in the aforementioned location, الحديث 139 الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 494 ey OlS yh Gat Hamdu Wa Huwa ‘Ala Kulli Shay’in Qadeer. It is the same whether you said it all together: Subhan Allahi WalHamdu Lillahi Wallahu Akbar thirty-three times, or you said the Tasbih by itself, the Tahmid by itself and the Takbir by itself and then completed it by saying: La Taha Illallahu Wahdahu La Shareeka Lahu, Lahul-Mulku Wa Lahul-Hamdu Wa Huwa ‘Ala Kulli Shay’in Qadeer. Likewise, it is مباح to make Tasbih, Tahmid and Takbir ten at a time, instead of making them thirty-three at a time, by saying: Subhan الله ten times, Al-Hamdu Lillah ten times and Allahu Akbar ten times, which comes to thirty; and this is what has been reported in السنة النبوية.) It has also been reported in السنة النبوية that one may say Subhan Allahi Wal-Hamdy Lillahi Wa La Ilaha Illallahu Wallahu Akbar, these four things are said twenty-five times, making the total one hundred.!?! So, whichever of these forms you used to make Tasbih, it is مباح, because the rule of the الإسلامي Law is that if acts of worship have been reported in various forms, it is a السنة to perform all of them, once this and once that, in order that a person may perform السنة النبوية in all its forms. These statements of remembrance which I mentioned are general for all of the prayers: Fajr, Zuhr, ‘Asr, Maghrib and ‘Isha’. And in "1 Reported by أبو داود in the Book of Good Manners, in the Chapter Regarding Tasbih at the Time of Sleeping, الحديث (5065); and it was reported by At-Tirmithi in the Book of Supplications, in Chapter 25, الحديث (3410); and by An-Nasa’i in the Book of Forgetfulness, in the Chapter: The Number of Tasbih After the Taslim (1347); and by ابن ماجه in the Book of Establishing the الصلاة, in the Chapter: What is Said After the Taslim (926). '] Reported by At-Tirmithi in the Book of Supplications, in Chapter 25 (3413) and he declared it to be صحيح and it was reported by AnNasa’i in the Book of Forgetfulness, in Chapter 93: Another Form of الفتاوى Relating to الصلاة 495 BLAS! sgl Maghrib and Fajr, the Tahlil''! is performed ten times, and likewise the words Rabbi Ajirni Min An-Nar (My الرب! Grant me protection from the Fire) are repeated seven times after the Maghrib and the Fajr prayers. And الله is the Granter of success. Q. 262. What is the ruling on raising the hands and supplicating after the الصلاة? A. It is not legislated for a person to raise his hands and supplicate after completing the الصلاة. If he wishes to supplicate, then الدعاء during the الصلاة is better than him supplicating after he finishes it. This is why النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # indicated that in الحديث النبوي الشريف of Ibn Mas’ud, رضي الله عنه, in which he mentioned the Tashahhud, saying:
